what_it_does: A RED→GREEN integration test for a sibling emit module: it builds a design-token ledger, calls emit.emit, and asserts the returned CSS contains imports, resolved primitive references, flat tokens, root_extra, and a generated header. It then mutates a primitive value and re-emits to prove that every token referencing it updates.
observations: Self-contained script with sys.path insertion to import the local emit module
Uses the real emit module and explicitly avoids mocks
Token ledger includes imports, root_extra, primitives, and grouped tokens
Asserts both primitive-reference resolution and flat-value emission
Mutates the input data in place to verify one-place-change propagation
Prints a PASS message on success
standouts: Includes a second-pass mutation assertion to prove one-place-change propagation
conventions: assert statements carry custom failure messages
Plain function runner instead of unittest/pytest
concerns: Mutates the input data dict between emission checks
Hardcodes expected CSS formatting with no whitespace tolerance
Assumes emit.emit returns a single CSS string
No explicit exit code or error handling on assertion failure
notes: A behavioral smoke test that treats the emitter as a black box and verifies primitive-reference propagation by editing the source value.
questions: What exact string format and selector structure does emit.emit produce?
Where is emit.py located and what is its full interface?
Do groups map to CSS selectors, scopes, or just namespaces?
How is root_extra rendered in the output CSS?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it is a RED→GREEN integration test for the emitter that checks real behavior.
intention_signals: Validate that the emitter can turn a token ledger into CSS while preserving reference semantics, so changing a primitive cascades to every token that references it.
novelty: The test goes beyond static output checks by mutating a primitive and asserting that all dependent tokens change, turning a design principle into a runtime proof.
contribution: A concrete behavioral contract for emit.emit covering imports, primitive resolution, flat tokens, root_extra, generated header, and one-place-change propagation.
summary_for_embedding: A RED→GREEN integration test for a local token emitter that verifies CSS output and primitive-reference propagation by mutating the source primitive.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the emitter resolves token references and propagates primitive changes to every referencing token without mocks.

symbols.does: Executes the RED→GREEN integration test for emit.emit.
symbols.description: Builds a token ledger with imports, primitives, groups, and root_extra; calls emit.emit and asserts the returned CSS contains the import line, resolved primitive references, a flat token, root_extra, and a generated header. It then mutates the primitive value and re-emits to verify that every token referencing it changes, printing PASS if all assertions succeed.
